tasks.description: Initialize Next.js project with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and project directory structure
Set up Firebase authentication (SDK, config, auth context, protected routes)
Set up OpenF1 API client with TypeScript types and data fetching utilities
Configure Vercel deployment, environment variables, and production build settings
Build session browsing UI: year, round, and session type selection with OpenF1 data
Build session detail page showing event info, circuit data, and available sessions
Implement Firebase Auth UI: sign-up, login, and account management pages
Implement user preferences (favorite sessions, watch history) with Firebase Firestore
Build Canvas-based track map rendering component from circuit coordinate data
Build race data loader: fetch telemetry, positions, and lap data from OpenF1 API per frame
Implement replay playback engine with play, pause, rewind, fast-forward, and speed controls
Build leaderboard component showing driver positions, lap counts, and status (OUT/racing)
Implement driver selection and focus on track map with telemetry overlay
Build driver telemetry HUD displaying speed, gear, throttle, brake, and DRS data
Build weather and session info panel showing track/air temp, humidity, wind, lap count, time
Implement race control messages feed and flag/status displays
Implement safety car visualization and pit stop event display
Build telemetry insights panel with per-driver charts (speed trace, gear analysis)
Implement responsive layout for mobile devices and touch-based replay controls
Add loading states, error boundaries, graceful error handling, and accessibility attributes
Performance optimization: lazy loading, canvas rendering optimization, bundle size reduction
Final Vercel deployment, environment verification, and README documentation
Initialize Next.js project with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and project directory structure
Set up Firebase authentication (SDK, config, auth context, protected routes)
Set up OpenF1 API client with TypeScript types and data fetching utilities
Configure Vercel deployment, environment variables, and production build settings
Build session browsing UI: year, round, and session type selection with OpenF1 data
Build session detail page showing event info, circuit data, and available sessions
Implement Firebase Auth UI: sign-up, login, and account management pages
Implement user preferences (favorite sessions, watch history) with Firebase Firestore
Build Canvas-based track map rendering component from circuit coordinate data
Build race data loader: fetch telemetry, positions, and lap data from OpenF1 API per frame
Implement replay playback engine with play, pause, rewind, fast-forward, and speed controls
Build leaderboard component showing driver positions, lap counts, and status (OUT/racing)
Implement driver selection and focus on track map with telemetry overlay
Build driver telemetry HUD displaying speed, gear, throttle, brake, and DRS data
Build weather and session info panel showing track/air temp, humidity, wind, lap count, time
Implement race control messages feed and flag/status displays
Implement safety car visualization and pit stop event display
Build telemetry insights panel with per-driver charts (speed trace, gear analysis)

Initialize Next.js project with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, ESLint, and app directory structure
tasks.acceptance: Next.js dev server starts without errors, TypeScript compiles, Tailwind styles render

tasks.description: Install and configure Firebase SDK, set up Firebase config files and client initialization
tasks.acceptance: Firebase app initializes without errors in browser console

tasks.description: Build Firebase Auth context provider with sign-up, login, logout, and session persistence
tasks.acceptance: Auth context provides user state and auth methods, persists across page refreshes

tasks.description: Implement ProtectedRoute wrapper component that redirects unauthenticated users
tasks.acceptance: Protected pages redirect to login when unauthenticated, render children when authenticated

tasks.description: Create OpenF1 API client module with base URL config, fetch utilities, and rate-limit handling (retry with backoff)
tasks.acceptance: API client can fetch from OpenF1 endpoints, handles rate limits with exponential backoff

tasks.description: Define TypeScript types and interfaces for all F1 data entities: Session, Driver, Lap, TelemetrySnapshot, TrackLayout, RaceEvent, WeatherReading
tasks.acceptance: All OpenF1 API responses map cleanly to TypeScript types with no `any` usage

tasks.description: Configure Vercel deployment with environment variables, build settings, and production-ready next.config
tasks.acceptance: npm run build completes with 0 errors, Vercel preview deploys automatically

tasks.description: Build session listing page: fetch available years, rounds, and session types from OpenF1 API and render as navigable hierarchy
tasks.acceptance: User can navigate year > round > session type in 3 clicks, sessions display event name and circuit

tasks.description: Build session detail page showing event info, circuit location, country, date, session status, and available replay actions
tasks.acceptance: Session detail displays all metadata, user can start replay or view session info

tasks.description: Build Firebase Auth UI pages: sign-up form, login form, and account settings page with password reset
tasks.acceptance: User can sign up, log in, reset password, and see their account info

tasks.description: Implement user preferences service with Firebase Firestore: save/load favorite sessions and watch history scoped to authenticated user
tasks.acceptance: Authenticated user can favorite a session and see it in their favorites list on return

tasks.description: Build Canvas-based track map rendering component that takes circuit coordinate array and draws the track layout with turn markers
tasks.acceptance: Track renders accurately from coordinate data, adapts to canvas size, shows turn labels

tasks.description: Build race data service: fetch telemetry, car positions, lap data, and weather from OpenF1 API keyed to session, organize into sequential frames
tasks.acceptance: Race data loads for a given session, frames contain all driver positions and telemetry per timestamp

tasks.description: Implement replay playback engine with frame buffering, play/pause toggle, rewind/fast-forward seek, and speed multiplier (0.5x, 1x, 2x, 4x)
tasks.acceptance: Replay plays forward at correct speed, pauses instantly, seeks to correct frame on rewind/forward

tasks.description: Build leaderboard component showing ordered driver list with position, number, name, team color, lap count, and retired (OUT) status
tasks.acceptance: Leaderboard updates every frame during replay, retired drivers show OUT status

tasks.description: Implement driver selection on track map and leaderboard, with telemetry overlay showing speed, gear, throttle, brake, DRS for selected driver
tasks.acceptance: Clicking a driver on track or leaderboard selects them, telemetry HUD updates per frame

tasks.description: Build weather and session info panel showing current lap, elapsed time, total laps, track temp, air temp, humidity, wind speed, and rainfall status
tasks.acceptance: Panel displays all weather/session fields and updates per frame during replay

tasks.description: Implement race control messages feed showing flags, safety car status, VSC, session status changes, and incident messages
tasks.acceptance: Messages appear in chronological order, flags and SC status reflected visually

tasks.description: Implement safety car visualization on track map (deploying/on-track/returning phases) and pit stop event display with timing and tyre compound
tasks.acceptance: Safety car appears on track during SC periods with phase transitions, pit stops show driver and tyre change

tasks.description: Build per-driver telemetry charts panel: speed trace over lap, gear distribution, throttle/brake timeline, synchronized with replay position
tasks.acceptance: Charts update in sync with replay, show accurate data for selected driver

tasks.description: Implement responsive CSS layout: mobile-first grid/stack layout for replay page, collapsible panels, touch-friendly tap targets
tasks.acceptance: All replay features usable on 375px mobile viewport without horizontal scroll

tasks.description: Add touch-based replay controls: tap/pinch/swipe gesture handlers for play/pause, seek, and driver selection on mobile
tasks.acceptance: Replay fully controllable via touch gestures on mobile devices

tasks.description: Add loading skeletons, error boundaries, graceful error handling for API failures, and ARIA accessibility attributes throughout
tasks.acceptance: API failures show user-friendly error state, keyboard navigation works, screen reader labels present

tasks.description: Performance optimization: lazy-load offscreen components, memoize canvas render loop, code-split heavy pages, optimize bundle size
tasks.acceptance: Lighthouse performance score >= 80 on mobile, bundle size within budget

tasks.description: Final Vercel deployment verification, environment variable audit, and README documentation with setup/usage instructions
tasks.acceptance: Vercel deployment succeeds, README documents setup steps and architecture

tasks.description: Initialize Next.js project with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, ESLint, and app directory structure — targets: package.json, tsconfig.json, next.config.js, tailwind.config.ts, app/layout.tsx, app/page.tsx
tasks.acceptance: Next.js dev server starts without errors, TypeScript compiles, Tailwind styles render

tasks.description: Define TypeScript types and interfaces for all F1 data entities: Session, Driver, Lap, TelemetrySnapshot, TrackLayout, RaceEvent, WeatherReading — targets: lib/types.ts
tasks.acceptance: All OpenF1 API response structures map to typed interfaces with no `any` usage

tasks.description: Create OpenF1 API client module with base URL config, fetch utilities, rate-limit handling (exponential backoff retry), and typed endpoint functions — targets: lib/api/openf1.ts
tasks.acceptance: API client can fetch from OpenF1 endpoints, handles rate limits, returns typed responses

tasks.description: Install and configure Firebase SDK, set up Firebase config file with project credentials and app initialization — targets: lib/firebase.ts
tasks.acceptance: Firebase app initializes without errors in browser console

tasks.description: Build Firebase Auth context provider with sign-up, login, logout, session persistence, and auth state hook — targets: lib/auth/AuthContext.tsx, lib/auth/useAuth.ts
tasks.acceptance: Auth context provides user state and auth methods, persists across page refreshes

tasks.description: Implement ProtectedRoute wrapper component and app/layout.tsx updates for route protection — targets: components/ProtectedRoute.tsx, app/layout.tsx
tasks.acceptance: Protected pages redirect to login when unauthenticated, render children when authenticated

tasks.description: Configure Vercel deployment with environment variable setup, production build settings in next.config, and vercel.json — targets: next.config.ts, vercel.json, .env.local.example
tasks.acceptance: npm run build completes with 0 errors, Vercel preview deploys automatically

tasks.description: Build session listing page: fetch available years, rounds, and session types from OpenF1 API, render as navigable hierarchy with loading states — targets: app/sessions/page.tsx, components/SessionBrowser.tsx
tasks.acceptance: User can navigate year > round > session type in 3 clicks, sessions display event name and circuit

tasks.description: Build session detail page showing event info, circuit location, country, date, session status, and replay button — targets: app/sessions/[year]/[round]/page.tsx, components/SessionDetail.tsx
tasks.acceptance: Session detail displays all metadata, user can start replay or view session info

tasks.description: Build Firebase Auth UI pages: sign-up form, login form, and account settings page with password reset — targets: app/auth/login/page.tsx, app/auth/signup/page.tsx, app/auth/account/page.tsx
tasks.acceptance: User can sign up, log in, reset password, and see their account info

tasks.description: Implement user preferences service with Firebase Firestore: save/load favorite sessions and watch history scoped to authenticated user — targets: lib/userPreferences.ts, components/FavoriteButton.tsx, components/WatchHistory.tsx
tasks.acceptance: Authenticated user can favorite a session and see it in their favorites list on return

tasks.description: Build Canvas-based track map rendering component that takes circuit coordinate array and draws track layout with turn markers and driver dots — targets: components/TrackMap.tsx, utils/trackRenderer.ts
tasks.acceptance: Track renders accurately from coordinate data, adapts to canvas size, shows turn labels

tasks.description: Build race data service: fetch telemetry, car positions, lap data, and weather from OpenF1 API keyed to session, organize into buffered frame sequence — targets: lib/raceData.ts, lib/frameBuffer.ts
tasks.acceptance: Race data loads for a given session, frames contain all driver positions and telemetry per timestamp

tasks.description: Implement replay playback engine with frame buffering, play/pause toggle, seek (rewind/fast-forward), and speed multiplier (0.5x, 1x, 2x, 4x) — targets: hooks/useReplayEngine.ts
tasks.acceptance: Replay plays forward at correct speed, pauses instantly, seeks to correct frame on rewind/forward

tasks.description: Build leaderboard component showing ordered driver list with position, number, name, team color, lap count, and retired (OUT) status overlay — targets: components/Leaderboard.tsx
tasks.acceptance: Leaderboard updates every frame during replay, retired drivers show OUT status

tasks.description: Implement driver selection on track map and leaderboard, with telemetry overlay HUD showing speed, gear, throttle, brake, DRS for selected driver — targets: components/DriverTelemetryHUD.tsx, hooks/useDriverSelection.ts
tasks.acceptance: Clicking a driver on track or leaderboard selects them, telemetry HUD updates per frame

tasks.description: Build weather and session info panel showing current lap, elapsed time, total laps, track temp, air temp, humidity, wind speed, rainfall — targets: components/WeatherPanel.tsx, components/SessionInfoBar.tsx
tasks.acceptance: Panel displays all weather/session fields and updates per frame during replay

tasks.description: Implement race control messages feed showing flags, safety car/VSC status, session status changes, and incident messages with timestamps — targets: components/RaceControlFeed.tsx
tasks.acceptance: Messages appear in chronological order, flags and SC status reflected visually

tasks.description: Implement safety car visualization (deploying/on-track/returning phases) on track map and pit stop event display showing driver, timing, and tyre compound — targets: components/SafetyCar.tsx, components/PitStopIndicator.tsx
tasks.acceptance: Safety car appears on track during SC periods with phase transitions, pit stops show driver and tyre change

tasks.description: Build per-driver telemetry charts panel: speed trace over lap, gear distribution visualization, throttle/brake timeline synchronized with replay position — targets: components/TelemetryCharts.tsx, utils/chartRenderer.ts
tasks.acceptance: Charts update in sync with replay, show accurate data for selected driver

tasks.description: Implement responsive mobile-first CSS layout for replay page: collapsible panels, stack layout, touch-friendly tap targets (min 44px), landscape optimization — targets: app/replay/[sessionId]/page.tsx, styles/replay.css
tasks.acceptance: All replay features usable on 375px mobile viewport without horizontal scroll

tasks.description: Add touch-based replay controls: tap to pause/play, swipe for seek, pinch for zoom on track map — targets: hooks/useTouchControls.ts, components/ReplayControls.tsx
tasks.acceptance: Replay fully controllable via touch gestures on mobile devices

tasks.description: Add loading skeletons, error boundaries, graceful error handling for API failures, and ARIA accessibility attributes throughout key components — targets: components/ErrorBoundary.tsx, components/LoadingSkeleton.tsx
tasks.acceptance: API failures show user-friendly error state, keyboard navigation works, screen reader labels present

tasks.description: Performance optimization: lazy-load offscreen panels, memoize canvas render loop via requestAnimationFrame, code-split heavy pages, optimize bundle with dynamic imports — targets: app/replay/[sessionId]/page.tsx, next.config.ts
tasks.acceptance: Lighthouse performance score >= 80 on mobile, bundle size within budget

tasks.description: Final Vercel deployment verification, environment variable audit, and README documentation with setup instructions and architecture overview — targets: README.md, vercel.json
tasks.acceptance: Vercel deployment succeeds, README documents setup steps and architecture

tasks.acceptance: npm run dev starts without errors, TypeScript compiles cleanly
